prompt:
Kiosk project, next cabinet: a Tetris-lite core. No RNG, no timers — the piece feed is a fixture string and gravity only happens when the script says tick, because we diff recorded frames between machines. Implement src/lib.rs in this crate (rust-tetralite); tests/tetralite.rs, Cargo.toml and .gitignore are read-only. Stdlib only.

Board 10 wide x 20 tall (also pub const WIDTH / HEIGHT). Game::new(feed: &str) -> Result<Game, String>: feed chars are the pieces in play order, from IOTSZJL; errors "empty feed" and "bad piece: X" (first offender). The first piece spawns immediately.

Pieces live in a box whose origin (top-left) spawns at row 0, col 3, and each piece has exactly 4 rotation states; rotating NEVER moves the origin and there are NO wall kicks. The tables, as (row, col) offsets in the box for states 0 (spawn), 1 (one clockwise turn), 2, 3:
I: 0:(1,0)(1,1)(1,2)(1,3) 1:(0,2)(1,2)(2,2)(3,2) 2:(2,0)(2,1)(2,2)(2,3) 3:(0,1)(1,1)(2,1)(3,1)
O: all four states (0,1)(0,2)(1,1)(1,2)
T: 0:(0,1)(1,0)(1,1)(1,2) 1:(0,1)(1,1)(1,2)(2,1) 2:(1,0)(1,1)(1,2)(2,1) 3:(0,1)(1,0)(1,1)(2,1)
S: 0:(0,1)(0,2)(1,0)(1,1) 1:(0,1)(1,1)(1,2)(2,2) 2:(1,1)(1,2)(2,0)(2,1) 3:(0,0)(1,0)(1,1)(2,1)
Z: 0:(0,0)(0,1)(1,1)(1,2) 1:(0,2)(1,1)(1,2)(2,1) 2:(1,0)(1,1)(2,1)(2,2) 3:(0,1)(1,0)(1,1)(2,0)
J: 0:(0,0)(1,0)(1,1)(1,2) 1:(0,1)(0,2)(1,1)(2,1) 2:(1,0)(1,1)(1,2)(2,2) 3:(0,1)(1,1)(2,0)(2,1)
L: 0:(0,2)(1,0)(1,1)(1,2) 1:(0,1)(1,1)(2,1)(2,2) 2:(1,0)(1,1)(1,2)(2,0) 3:(0,0)(0,1)(1,1)(2,1)
Only the four CELLS matter for bounds and collisions — the box itself may hang past an edge (a vertical I can stand in column 0).

step(&mut self, cmd: char) -> Result<(), String>, commands: 'L'/'R' shift one column, 'C' clockwise turn, 'A' counter-clockwise, 'D' gravity tick (down one row), 'H' hard drop. A shift or rotation that would collide or leave the board is IGNORED (Ok, nothing changes). A 'D' that can't descend LOCKS the piece instead; 'H' descends as far as possible and locks. On lock: full rows disappear, rows above shift down, score += [0,100,300,500,800][rows cleared] (pub const SCORES), lines += cleared; then the next feed piece spawns at origin (0,3) state 0. If the feed is empty the game ends with Status::Complete; if the fresh spawn collides with the stack it ends with Status::ToppedOut (the piece is not placed). Unknown commands: "bad command: x". Any step after the game ends: "game over".

Also: pub enum Status { Playing, Complete, ToppedOut } (derive Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq), status(), is_over(), score() -> u32, lines() -> u32, run(&mut self, script: &str) -> Result<usize, String> feeding every non-whitespace char to step and wrapping failures as "step 2 (x): bad command: x" (1-based), and render() -> String: 20 rows of 10 chars joined with \n, '#' locked, '@' the falling piece, '.' empty, no trailing newline.

The tests replay scripted move/tick sequences and compare whole frames: every piece is spun through all four states against the tables above (both directions), walls and floor pin the no-kick/ignore rules, and four staged games land a single, a double, a triple and a tetris with exact scores and after-clear frames. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
